response:
Robert M. Fomon was **Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of E. F. Hutton & Co.** from 1970 to 1987, and he was **Governor of the New York Stock Exchange**. He also served as **Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Pacific Stock Exchange**. 

Fomon's connection to the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) at 11 Wall Street, Lower Manhattan, New York City, was that he held significant executive positions within the exchange, including governance roles and leadership in the company that operated the exchange. His tenure as Governor of the NYSE and his leadership in E. F. Hutton & Co. placed him at the center of the exchange's operations and governance.
